records.ety: [OE. dasen, prob. from Icel. dasask to become weary, a reflexive verb; cf. Sw. dasa to lie idle, and OD. daesen to be foolish, insane, daes, dwaes, D. dwaas, foolish, insane, AS. dwǣs, dysig, stupid. √71. Cf. Dizzy, Doze.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Dazed (dāzd); p. pr. & vb. n. Dazing.]

defs: To stupefy with excess of light; with a blow, with cold, or with fear; to confuse; to benumb.
quotes: While flashing beams do daze his feeble eyen.
Such souls, Whose sudden visitations daze the world.
He comes out of the room in a dazed state, that is an odd though a sufficient substitute for interest.

defs: The state of being dazed; as, he was in a daze.
